title: Tender Dutch Soya Bean Stew
description: A spicy and indulgent Dutch dish featuring soya bean, slow-cooked to perfection in an agave-leaf vessel, this stew is a hearty and comforting meal. It is kosher-friendly and made with wholesome ingredients.
instructions: Rinse the dried soya beans and soak them in water for at least 8 hours or overnight.
Drain and rinse the soya beans, then set them aside.
In the agave-leaf vessel, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the chopped carrot and celery and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the tomato paste, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the soaked soya beans, vegetable broth, and 2 cups of water to the vessel, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2 hours, or until the soya beans are tender.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
